content: Hey, I'm Rowan, nice to meet you. I've been driving trucks for over 20 years, and I'm finally training for a triathlon next month.
Nice to meet you too, Rowan! Congratulations on deciding to train for a triathlon, that's a big accomplishment. What made you decide to start training?
I've always been interested in speedcubing, and I figured why not try something new and challenging. Plus, I've been feeling a bit sedentary from all the driving, so I needed to get moving.
Speedcubing is a unique hobby, I've never met anyone who's into it before. How long have you been speedcubing, and what's your fastest time?
I've been speedcubing for about 5 years now, and my fastest time is around 20 seconds. I've also been getting into gardening, I find it really relaxing after a long drive.
20 seconds is impressive! Gardening is a great way to unwind, what kind of plants do you like to grow?
I've got a small garden in my backyard in Dallas, and I grow all sorts of things - tomatoes, cucumbers, carrots. I've also been trying to learn more about beekeeping, it's really fascinating.
Beekeeping is a great hobby, it's so important for the environment. Have you considered getting a beehive for your garden?
Actually, I've already got a beehive, and it's been doing really well. I've been harvesting honey and giving it to my friends and family as gifts.
That's wonderful! There's nothing like homemade honey. What kind of training are you doing for your triathlon, and how's it going?
I've been doing a lot of running and cycling, and I've also been swimming at the local pool. It's been tough, but I'm determined to finish the triathlon on June 15th.
You're doing great, Rowan! It's amazing that you're pushing yourself to finish the triathlon. Have you thought about what you'll do after you finish?
Actually, I've been thinking about starting a food blog, I love cooking Japanese cuisine and trying out new recipes.
A food blog sounds like a great idea, you could share all your favorite recipes with your friends and family. Have you thought about what you'll name your blog?
I've been thinking about that, maybe something like 'The Truck Driver's Kitchen' or 'Rowan's Ramen Recipes'. What do you think?
I think both of those names are great, but you could also try to come up with something that incorporates your love of speedcubing and triathlons.
That's a great idea, I'll have to brainstorm some more. Maybe I'll even start a YouTube channel to go along with my blog.
A YouTube channel would be a great way to share your recipes and cooking techniques with a wider audience. You could even do some videos on speedcubing and triathlon training.
I'll definitely consider it, thanks for the advice. I'm looking forward to finishing my triathlon and starting this new chapter in my life.
You're welcome, Rowan! It was great talking to you, and I wish you all the best with your triathlon and your future endeavors.
